text: The surgeon induces pneumoperitoneum using the open Hasson technique and inserts three or four trocars, similar to a left laparoscopic colectomy setup. The peritoneal cavity is assessed for the grade of peritonitis and Hinchey stage, ensuring fecal peritonitis is excluded. If a perforation site is visible, the surgeon attempts laparoscopic suturing.
The surgeon performs copious lavage of all abdominal quadrants, followed by the insertion of one or two abdominal drains.

The surgeon performs a single incision laparoscopic sigmoid resection through a three-centimeter transumbilical skin incision. A multiport device is inserted, and the sigmoid colon is resected using a harmonic scalpel.
The surgeon performs laparoscopic adhesiolysis, using either sharp dissection with cold scissors or a harmonic scalpel, or gentle blunt dissection to separate adhesions within the abdominal cavity.

The surgeon performs a single incision sigmoidectomy with intracorporeal anastomosis, extracting the specimen through the umbilical incision. The surgical site shows a three-centimeter umbilical scar post-procedure.
The surgeon performs a sigmoid resection using a flexible endo stapler for distal resection, with visualization provided by a 5mm laparoscopic camera.

The surgeon performs a wide mobilization of the descending colon from the left paracolic gutter, a crucial step in the single incision sigmoidectomy. This mobilization facilitates the distal stapled edge of the colon reaching the umbilicus for extraction through a wound protector, enabling proximal resection.

The surgeon extracts the specimen through the umbilical incision using a wound protector. The proximal section is completed with anvil insertion. Following reinduction of pneumoperitoneum and mobilization of the descending colon, an intracorporeal anastomosis is created using a circular stapler inserted transanally.

The surgeon verifies the correct orientation of the proximal colon, ensuring the mesentery is not twisted. The surgical field shows no abdominal drains, and a hydronomatic test is performed to confirm the integrity of the anastomosis.
